seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Mastra + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Mastra + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building reliable data extraction agents with Mastra enables schema-guided extraction from unstructured documents, powering intelligent document processing workflows. Vex guardrails are essential to catch malformed outputs, validate schema compliance, and prevent stale context from derailing extraction accuracy. This integration guide shows how to add Vex middleware to your Mastra pipelines for production-grade reliability.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Mastra
architectureOverview.description:
Mastra's agent workflows orchestrate document ingestion, chunking, embedding storage, and schema-guided LLM extraction. The pipeline typically starts with file upload to a knowledge base, followed by retrieval-augmented generation where agents fetch relevant chunks and invoke structured LLM calls. Tools handle parsing, validation, and integration with external systems like CRMs or databases.

The core extraction step uses Mastra's workflow nodes with Pydantic-like schemas to enforce output structure. Agents transition between validation, enrichment, and persistence steps, with memory managing conversation context across multi-turn extractions. Failure often occurs when LLM outputs deviate from schemas or retrieval injects irrelevant context.

Vex integrates as middleware in Mastra's agent executor, wrapping tool calls, workflow transitions, and LLM invocations to validate outputs against extraction schemas before state updates.

risks.name: Workflow step outputs not validated between transitions
risks.description: Mastra workflows pass unvalidated LLM outputs between nodes, causing downstream parsing failures
risks.mitigation: Vex schema guards on workflow transitions enforce type consistency

risks.name: Agent tool calls returning malformed data
risks.description: Document parsing tools return inconsistent formats that break extraction schemas
risks.mitigation: Vex tool output validators catch malformed data before agent reasoning

risks.name: Memory retrieval injecting stale context
risks.description: Old document versions in Mastra memory confuse extraction agents
risks.mitigation: Vex context freshness checks filter stale retrievals

risks.name: Sync integration data causing agent confusion
risks.description: External API responses with unexpected fields disrupt structured extraction
risks.mitigation: Vex field-level accuracy guards on integration outputs
